audit_items.proof_trace: add_close_mapping() contains a single statement: mappings.push_back(Mapping(node->pstate() + node->pstate().offset, current_position)). Every type involved (ParserState, Position, Offset, Mapping) is a pure value type composed entirely of size_t fields and non-owning const char* pointers — zero explicit heap allocations occur. The std::vector::push_back may internally reallocate, but this is fully RAII-managed; a std::bad_alloc propagates cleanly with no leak. The property's antecedent is vacuously satisfied: no allocations exist, so no memory leak is possible.
